attacking_style: Launches final climb attacks from reduced groups, typically after domestique leadout. Excels in breakaways on mountain stages, converting positioning into stage wins rather than sustained GC efforts.
team_tactics: AG2R sacrifices breakaway teammates to set up his attacks — SCHÄR led him out at Tour de Suisse, O'Connor sacrificed break position at Tour de France to launch him perfectly on stage 17.
terrain_preference: Mountain stage finishes from breakaways or reduced groups. Struggles badly on descents and time trials — caught by rider starting 2min behind in TT, dropped by SKJELMOSE group on descent despite holding yellow.
vulnerability: Pure climber limitations exposed in TTs and technical descents. Cannot defend when isolated or respond to accelerations without team support, as shown losing Tour de Suisse yellow when caught alone.
key_patterns: AG2R sacrifices domestiques in breaks/climbs to launch his attacks (3 observations)
Converts runner-up finishes and near-misses into wins with improved execution (2 observations)
Struggles when isolated from team support, vulnerable on descents (2 observations)
Thrives in breakaways on hardest mountain stages rather than GC battles (2 observations)
Time trial weakness confirms pure climber profile, loses minutes to GC riders (2 observations)
form_trajectory: positive: Won Tour de France stage 17 after earlier success at Tour de Suisse, showing progression from near-misses to converting opportunities despite GC limitations exposed mid-Suisse
